manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: match, ring, genius, needle, plastic, octopus, whip, yard, paste.
Your opponent's team words are: board, mount, casino, bug, olive, mole, box, fan.
Distractor words are: duck, lemon, pit, wind, tail, palm, tie, cast.
The kill words are: .

message.content:
.CLUE: MATERIAL
TARGETS: plastic, needle, ring, paste
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue and list of target words from our team that the clue relates to. Cannot be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must be semantically related. Need to avoid kill words; there are none listed? The kill words are: . (empty). So no kill words.

We should pick clue that covers multiple of our words. Our team words: match, ring, genius, needle, plastic, octopus, whip, yard, paste.

We need to avoid opponent words and distractor words being associated inadvertently. But we only list target words we intend.

We need a clue that connects several: for example "SEWING" relates to needle, paste? Paste maybe glue. Whip? Not. Octopus? Not. Ring? Not. Could use "CLOTHES" - needle, paste? Actually paste not. Could use "CIRCUIT" - match (circuit breaker), ring (circuit ring), genius (electric? No). Could use "SPOOL" - needle thread, paste? hmm.

We can aim for 3-4 words.

Consider "FISHING": octopus (sea creature), whip (whip line), yard (yardage?), match (match hook?), ring (ring? fishing ring?), plastic (plastic lures). Not great.

Consider "CLOTH": needle, paste (fabric paste?), whip (whip? maybe not), ring (clothing ring?), match (match? no).

Consider "SCIENTIFIC": genius, needle (lab), plastic (material), match (chemical match?), octopus (study). Might be too broad.

We need a single word not containing any part of any listed words. For example "LAB". Could relate to genius (lab genius), needle (lab equipment), plastic (lab materials), octopus (lab study). Also "match" maybe lab match? Not.

Alternatively "INNOVATION": genius, plastic, octopus? Not.

We could do two separate clues but instruction is provide one clue. So choose a clue that strongly links several words.

Perhaps "SEA": octopus, ring? No. "WHIP": not.

Maybe "CRAFT": needle (craft), paste (craft glue), whip (craft? maybe not), ring (craft jewelry), match (craft match?), plastic (craft material). Could work.

But "craft" is not an opponent or distractor word. Good.

List target words we think related: needle, paste, plastic, ring, whip? maybe.

But to be safe pick few: needle, paste, plastic.

Clue: "DIY". That relates to needle (sewing), paste (glue), plastic (craft). Good.

Check "DIY" is a word? It's abbreviation. Could be okay. Possibly better "SEWING" relates to needle, paste? Paste maybe not. "GLUE" relates to paste only. Hmm.

We need at least one target. Could give clue "SEWING": needle, whip? Not. Better "ARTS": needle (needlework), paste (collage), plastic (sculpture), ring (jewelry), whip? maybe not.

Maybe "MATERIAL": plastic, needle (metal), ring (metal), paste (adhesive). Could be.

But we need to avoid ambiguous to opponent words. Opponent words: board, mount, casino, bug, olive, mole, box, fan. Our clue "MATERIAL" not overlapping.

Let's propose clue "MATERIAL". Targets: plastic, needle, ring, paste.

Check each: plastic is a material, needle is made of metal material, ring (metal material), paste is a material. Good.

Will partner guess them correctly? Possibly.

Thus answer format:

CLUE: MATERIAL
TARGETS: plastic, needle, ring, paste

Make sure no extra text
